summary: This notebook demonstrates a complete training pipeline for 3D cryo-electron tomography (CryoET) object segmentation using the DeepFindET library. It walks through configuring a CoPick project to define protein targets and background classes, generating volumetric segmentation masks, and initializing a Residual U-Net for multi-class segmentation. The author highlights how to handle severe class imbalance by manually assigning class weights during training. Readers learn the end-to-end workflow for preparing 3D tomographic data and training a custom 3D CNN without writing low-level training loops.
competition_flow: Configures a CoPick project to load 3D CryoET tomograms and segmentation masks, generates training targets, and trains a 3D Residual U-Net with class-weighted loss to segment multiple protein classes and membranes.
data_reading: Loads 3D tomographic volumes and segmentation masks via the copick library from a JSON configuration file.
Extracts specific Run-IDs and applies a fixed voxel size (10 Å) to retrieve aligned volumetric data.
data_processing: Copies and renames overlay files to match CoPick conventions.
Generates multi-class segmentation masks via step1.create_train_targets.
Extracts fixed 72x72x72 voxel input cubes for the network.
insights: CoPick provides a standardized way to manage 3D CryoET projects and segmentation targets without manual file path management.
DeepFindET abstracts the 3D CNN training loop, allowing focus on data configuration and hyperparameters.
Explicit class weighting is necessary to handle the extreme imbalance between rare protein targets and background/membrane classes.
